text: When working with APIs, you typically send HTTP requests to a server to get or send data. Most APIs use JSON, a simple text format that both humans and machines can read easily. Before accessing many APIs, you’ll need authentication, often using an API key to identify yourself. It’s also important to respect rate limits, which prevent you from making too many requests too quickly. Finally, always implement error handling. APIs can return errors for many reasons, such as bad input or server issues. Checking the response status codes helps you respond correctly.
